conjecture_statement: Let $G=(V, E)$ be a simple graph. Let $V_\\Delta(G) \\subseteq V$ be the set of vertices incident to at least one triangle in $G$. Let $G_\\Delta = G[V_\\Delta(G)]$ be the subgraph of $G$ induced by $V_\\Delta(G)$. For a random graph $G \\sim G(n, p)$ with $p=c/n$, the 3-core of $G_\\Delta$ is non-empty asymptotically almost surely if and only if $c > c_3$, where $c_3 = \\inf_{x>0} \\frac{x}{1 - (1+x)e^{-x}} \\approx 3.3507$. That is, $\\lim_{n \\to \\infty} \\mathbb{P}(\\text{core}_3(G_\\Delta) \\neq \\emptyset) = \\begin{cases} 0 & c < c_3 \\\\ 1 & c > c_3 \\end{cases}$.
lean4_sketch:
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Core
import Mathlib.Analysis.SpecialFunctions.Exp
import Mathlib.Topology.Basic

open Real Finset SimpleGraph

universe u

/- The critical constant c₃ for the 3-core threshold. -/
noncomputable def c3_threshold_func (x : ℝ) : ℝ :=
  x / (1 - (1+x) * exp(-x))

lemma c3_saddle_point_exists : ∃ x₀ > 0, IsMinOn (c3_threshold_func) (Set.Ioi 0) x₀ := by
  sorry -- Existence of the minimum requires analysis of the derivative.

noncomputable def c3 : ℝ := Classical.choose (c3_saddle_point_exists)

-- Definition of the subgraph induced by triangle vertices
def triangle_vertices (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : Finset (Fin n) :=
  G.support.filter (fun v => (G.subgraph (G.neighborFinset v)).edgeSet.Nonempty)

def triangle_induced_subgraph (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n)) : SimpleGraph (Fin n) :=
  G.subgraph (triangle_vertices G).toSet

/--
Conjecture: The threshold for the emergence of a 3-core in G(n, c/n) is determined
by the subgraph induced by vertices belonging to triangles.
-/
theorem kcore_threshold_c3
    (c : ℝ) (ε : ℝ) (hε : ε > 0)
    (P : (SimpleGraph (Fin n) → Prop)) -- Placeholder for an event in G(n, p)
    : ∃ (N : ℕ), ∀ n ≥ N,
    let p := (c/n : NNReal); -- Placeholder for probability space
    if c > c3 + ε then
      -- Probability of non-empty 3-core in the triangle subgraph > 1-ε
      1 - ε < sorry
    else if c < c3 - ε then
      -- Probability of non-empty 3-core in the triangle subgraph < ε
      sorry < ε
    else True :=
by
  intro c ε hε P N -- Assume n is large enough
  -- The proof would analyze the number of graphs with m = cn/2 edges.
  -- Let T_G be the triangle_induced_subgraph of G.
  -- We need to show that |{G : |V|=n, |E|=m | core 3 T_G ≠ ∅}| / |{G : |V|=n, |E|=m}| → 1 or 0.

  -- Sub-Lemma 1: Counting rooted, triangle-based structures.
  -- This would involve a bijection between a set of graphs and a set of decorated trees,
  -- which is a standard technique in analytic combinatorics.
